channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Vertafore Launches Velocity AI Platform Agents
item.description: Vertafore (Denver) has introduced its Velocity AI Platform and a portfolio of six AI agents designed to support insurance workflows for agencies, managing general agents and carriers. And because they’re all built on the same foundation of the Velocity AI platform, they can evolve and connect over time in ways that make the entire system more powerful.” Vertafore says the platform supports its focus on improving distribution processes by reducing operational friction and embedding AI into day-to-day activities across the insurance lifecycle. Vertafore says the platform serves as the foundation for its AI capabilities across its product portfolio, including AgencyOne, MGA solutions and Sircon.

item.title: Tokio Marine HCC Specialty Group Selects Origami Risk Platform
item.description: Tokio Marine HCC-Specialty Group (TMHCC, Houston) has selected Origami Risk (Chicago) to provide its cloud-native property/casualty platform supporting policy, billing and claims operations across its global specialty portfolio. Origami Risk’s platform is purpose‑built to help carriers operate more efficiently, respond quickly to market changes, and deliver outstanding service to partners and policyholders.” Tokio Marine HCC-Specialty Group is part of Tokio Marine Group, which operates globally across specialty insurance markets. Tokio Marine HCC-Specialty Group will implement Origami Risk’s full suite to streamline workflows, improve speed to market and support service delivery across specialty lines.

item.title: ‘Essential to diversify’: Taiping Re looks beyond property cat as market softens, doubles down on Hong Kong, health
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sompo bolsters Australia underwriting team in major expansion drive
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Rare Earth Insurance revamps leadership across Asia and Europe
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Fire Departments Halt Service Due to Lapse in Workers’ Compensation
item.description: Last week, two volunteer fire departments in Vandergrift Borough, Pennsylvania suspended their services because their workers’ compensation insurance policies lapsed. State law requires fire departments to have the coverage. However, according to Mayor Lenny Collini, the borough arranged for backup from neighboring fire departments for “uninterrupted emergency response coverage” until the insurance issue is fixed.

item.title: Vertafore launches ReferenceConnect AI, powered by Velocity AI, to transform how insurance professionals access trusted knowledge
item.description: The post Vertafore launches ReferenceConnect AI, powered by Velocity AI, to transform how insurance professionals access trusted knowledge appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . New AI-powered experience delivers up to 80% faster answers, combining verified insurance content with the speed and ease of natural language search Vertafore announced the launch of ReferenceConnect AI , an AI-powered experience built on the Vertafore Velocity AI Platform , to transform how insurance professionals access and apply trusted industry knowledge in their everyday work. That’s how we’re applying AI to remove friction and help the industry move toward greater Distribution Velocity.” ReferenceConnect AI reflects Vertafore’s strategy to embed AI directly into the workflows where insurance work happens—turning trusted knowledge into faster decisions.

item.title: Sedgwick takes full ownership of Dutch injury assistance JV
item.description: Sedgwick has acquired the remaining minority stake in Dé Letselschade Hulpdienst (LSHD), taking full ownership of the Netherlands-based personal injury assistance provider after more than a decade as a joint venture. The stake was purchased from Raasveld Holding, giving Sedgwick full control of a business that supports injured individuals immediately following an accident. Sedgwick said the move strengthens its position in the Netherlands and allows it to further address gaps in the early stages of the claims and recovery process, where coordination and timely support can influence outcomes.

item.title: Albertsons Reaches $774 Million Opioid Accord
item.description: announced a $774 million settlement to resolve all opioid-related claims, making the grocery chain one of the last major retailers to reach such a deal. Albertsons is the latest big retailer to reach a settlement in the wake of the opioid crisis. Due to the settlement, the company recorded a charge of about $600 million last quarter, net of tax, that left it reporting a loss of roughly $480 million.

item.title: Millions of Rockstar Games Business Records Stolen, Hackers Say
item.description: A hacking group with a history of compromising major global corporations said it had stolen nearly 80 million business records from video game developer Rockstar Games, the maker of Grand Theft Auto. A representative of the hacking group told Reuters in an online chat on Monday it has 78.6 million records from Rockstar’s account with Snowflake, a large corporate data management company. Rockstar’s Snowflake data was accessed via a compromise of data from Anodot, an AI-powered business analytics platform, the hacking group representative said.

item.title: Verisk: Claims Volumes Fell to Five-Year Low in 2025 as Emerging Risks Intensify
item.description: Insurance claims volumes in the United States declined across most lines in 2025, but risks remain elevated as insurers navigate complex and concentrated claims, according to an annual report from Verisk. Claims volumes were lower in 2025 than 2024 across personal and commercial lines, Verisk’s Annual Insurance Claims Trends Report found. There were 5.3 million homeowners claims in 2025, the lowest level in the past five years and down 19% year-over-year from 6.5 million in 2024.

item.title: IBHS Wildfire Prepared Program Expands to 10 More States
item.description: Expanding our Wildfire Prepared designation program allows more families and neighbors to take coordinated, science-based action to reduce risk and strengthen resilience.” Established in 2022, Wildfire Prepared is built on the latest IBHS research examining how homes ignite during wildfire events and what mitigation measures most effectively interrupt structure-to-structure spread. Fourteen – that’s the total number of states that will now have access to the Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety’s Wildfire Prepared designation program. The research-based wildfire mitigation program brings science-based wildfire mitigation and third-party verification to more property owners in high-risk regions.

item.title: Reinsurers Stand Apart as M&A Buyers; Many Re Deals Destroy Value: Analysis
item.description: The report, “Carrier Mergers & Acquisitions: Drivers, Implications & Outcomes,” published by the insurance industry standards-setting body ACORD , notes that publicly traded reinsurers and multilines (writing both property/casualty and life/health insurance) buyers in M&A deals stand apart from P/C and L/H carriers, who are largely value creators. A recently released analysis of insurance carrier and reinsurer mergers and acquisitions finds that 75% of recent deals featuring reinsurers and multiline insurers as buyers destroyed value for shareholders. 31, 2025, and indexed against the MSCI World Index to isolate deal-specific outcomes from broader equity market movements.) Related article : Nearly Half of 100 Largest P/C Insurers Destroy Value: ACORD As a group, reinsurers and multiline carriers produced 13.3% lower returns than the market between close date and year-end 2025, while post-deal returns for P/C insurers were 3.1% above the overall equity market.

item.title: Despite Reduced Claims Volume, Complex Risks Grow: Report
item.description: Though insurance claims declined in 2025, underlying risks became more complex and concentrated, leading to more severe and challenging losses for many insurers, according to its annual ClaimsSearch Trends Report. Lower claims volume in 2025 masks shifting risks from emerging trends, wildfires, and targeted auto theft, according to Verisk, a data analytics and tech provider to the global insurance market. Commercial auto claims volume fell 5 percent in 2025 but remained 14 percent higher than in 2021, reflecting longer-term expansion of commercial driving risk, the report noted.

item.title: Collateralised fronting the centrepiece of Hannover Re’s broad ILS offering: Sehm & Horstmann
item.description: While Hannover Re has expanded its range of insurance-linked securities services and facilitation, responding to market demand, the company sees collateralised fronting as the centrepiece of this capital market focused aspect of its global reinsurance operations, executives Silke Sehm and Patrick Horstmann told us in an interview. Now that new division is operational, we spoke with Hannover Re Executive Board Member Silke Sehm and Head of ILS Patrick Horstmann, who stressed that the reinsurer wants to be a dependable and scalable partner for the ILS market and its constituents, across all the different offerings. “Our role is to act as a facilitator, offering a dependable and scalable set of solutions that work for both cedants and investors across market cycles.” Moving on to discuss the recent Bermuda unit launch, Sehm explained how it rounds out Hannover Re’s offering in ILS, “With the successful launch of our Bermuda-based sidecar structure, Hannover Re Capital Partners, HCP, we now provide services for the full spectrum of ILS instruments.

item.title: Convex targets $200m of retrocession with its fourth Hypatia catastrophe bond
item.description: Convex Group, the expansive global specialty insurance and reinsurance underwriter, has returned to the catastrophe bond market and has an initial target to secure $200 million or more in retrocession from its third catastrophe bond sponsorship, a Hypatia Ltd. Convex became a first-time catastrophe bond sponsor back in 2020, securing $300 million of protection from the capital markets with its first Hypatia deal . As a result, Convex currently has just the $150 million from its Series 2025-1 Hypatia cat bond in-force, so it’s good to see the re/insurer back and looking to expand on its capital markets retrocession.

item.title: AI treated as force-multiplier for cyber losses. Introduces aggregation, correlation risks: CyberCube
item.description: The insurance and reinsurance industry is currently treating artificial intelligence (AI) as a potential “force-multiplier” for cyber losses, but the pace of development of AI and its roll-out is reshaping cyber risk in general and introduces portfolio aggregation and loss correlation risks, specialist modeller CyberCube has explained. In that scenario, dependencies across compute, cloud, and model providers could act as shared points of failure, requiring explicit modeling of how disruptions at these layers might propagate across insured portfolios and generate correlated, cross-sector losses.” CyberCube notes that artificial intelligence (AI) is currently “treated by (re)insurers as a force multiplier within existing loss frameworks rather than a distinct risk class.” That view is now being challenged as AI evolves and attack timelines compress, while emerging developments such as identity-layer propagation and the proliferation of AI Agents add to the uncertainty around this approach. “As a result, recovery capability may become a more important determinant of business interruption (BI) loss severity than traditional preventative controls.” CyberCube calls on cyber catastrophe and aggregation risk modellers to incorporate specific AI risk dynamics into their thinking and output.

item.title: M&A activity and reinsurers’ pressure could quickly cool aviation rate reductions: WTW
item.description: Further major losses, increased mergers and acquisition (M&A) activity, or pressure from reinsurers could reduce capacity across the sector which is likely to cool rate reductions quickly, WTW’s ‘General Aviation Insurance Market Outlook: Q1 2026’ warns. The general aviation insurance market appears to have started the year in a state of relative stability, with abundant capacity continuing to drive a competitive rate environment While the market offers opportunities for underwriters to diversify their aviation portfolios, a string of losses in the airline sector during 2025 brought the entire industry under increased scrutiny. This tension has resulted in an abundance of capacity in the general aviation market which is creating competition for inclusion on well managed insurance programs.

item.title: Kin adds Okta’s CFO Brett Tighe to Board of Directors
item.description: Kin, the direct-to-consumer digital home and auto insurance and home finance provider, has appointed Brett Tighe, the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) of Okta, to its Board of Directors. In his role at Okta, Tighe oversees the firm’s financial strategy that guided it from its early stages through IPO and into its current position as a globally recognised, multibillion-dollar public company. As we continue to expand our home insurance, auto insurance, and home financing products, Brett’s experience will help us build solutions our customers can confidently rely on for the long term.” Tighe added, “Trust is earned when customers know they can rely on you to do what you say you will.

item.title: EU‑wide risk‑sharing framework to help address nat cat insurance protection gap: Morningstar DBRS
item.description: The European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority (EIOPA) and the European Stability Mechanism (ESM), have announced a proposal for a Europe‑wide risk‑sharing framework to address the natural catastrophe insurance protection gap while safeguarding private‑sector involvement. “We consider that the adoption of an EU‑wide public‑private insurance scheme would further support the reduction of the natural catastrophe insurance protection gap and help mitigate risks related to insurance affordability and availability across Europe,” Morningstar DBS’ stated. From a credit perspective, Morningstar DBRS sees the initiative as incrementally positive for the private insurance sector, particularly as it is designed to complement existing national schemes rather than replace them.

item.title: Trucordia Acquires Louisiana’s JJL&W Insurance Consulting
item.description: Trucordia announced its acquisition of the assets of JJL&W Insurance Consulting, an employee benefits brokerage based in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. JJL&W was founded in 2020 by partners Jeff Joffrion, Troy Jones, Mitch Lansing, and Jeff Wampold, The JJL&W team focuses on employee benefits for employers of all sizes, as well as individuals, with medical, dental, vision, life, disability and voluntary benefits, along with support for funding strategies, compliance, and administration.

item.title: Farmers Face Local Opposition to Host Solar
item.description: “It wasn’t a scary picture that everybody likes to paint about solar and the loss of farmland.” Local opposition to solar has long been an obstacle for green energy developers. But some communities are working to reverse local restrictions, citing the tax benefits and jobs the projects bring and the lease payments from energy companies that can provide stable income to farmers in a volatile industry. Greier would be worrying less if the solar farm he wanted on his land had come to pass.

item.title: Tesla’s Gigafactory Water Use Surges in Austin as New Chip Plant Looms
item.description: Tesla’s Giga Texas increased its annual treated water use by more than 200 million gallons in just two years, raising concerns about whether Austin can fulfill its long-term water conservation plans as Elon Musk advances plans for a new semiconductor plant expected to push demand even higher. From 2023 to 2025, Tesla’s annual treated water use rose about 68% to 556 million gallons becoming Austin Water’s third-largest customer — up from fifth in 2023, according to Austin Water. “All of a sudden, they’re using more water than the vast majority of people in the city.” Tesla began construction of the Gigafactory in the summer 2020, with the help of $13.9 million tax rebate from Travis County.

item.title: Geo Underwriting secures Intact Insurance capacity deal to scale marine trade offering
item.description: The post Geo Underwriting secures Intact Insurance capacity deal to scale marine trade offering appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Geo Underwriting has announced a new capacity partnership with Intact Insurance, set to launch on 1 May 2026, marking a further expansion of its specialist marine trade offering. “Intact already operates successfully within this highly specialised insurance market and the team’s clear insight into Geo’s underwriting requirements and commitment to fully supporting the development of our proposition gives us the autonomy required to continue to deliver the unique attributes that underpin Geo’s value to brokers; namely our own wordings, our defined underwriting appetite, and our specialist claims propositions.” Adrian Scott added: “Ultimately, brokers and their clients are at the very heart of everything we do, and this move provides our specialist underwriting team with enhanced tools and autonomy to drive forward our commercial objectives, and offer our broker partners an even stronger suite of Geo Marine products.” Steve Hedge said: “In a market where consistency of capacity and underwriting discipline are increasingly important, this partnership with Geo allows us to support brokers with a more stable and specialist marine offering.

item.title: Intelligent AI expands into US market with property risk intelligence platform
item.description: Intelligent AI has announced its expansion into the US market, following its selection for the Connecticut Insurtech Corridor, as it looks to address persistent data challenges in property underwriting. The company’s platform delivers high-resolution COPE (Construction, Occupancy, Protection, Exposure) data and rebuild cost intelligence, providing insurers with a structured and consistent view of property risk at the point of underwriting. Anthony Peake said: “Expanding into the US is a natural next step for Intelligent AI, and being selected for the Connecticut InsurTech Corridor gives us a powerful platform to engage directly with leading US insurers.

item.title: Better launches ChatGPT-based mortgage underwriting engine
item.description: Better.com launched a conversational credit decision engine in ChatGPT for mortgage and home equity lenders, giving enterprise users access to its Tinman AI platform through a custom connector built with OpenAI. Better said the platform is trained on more than $110 billion in funded loans, 12 million recorded customer calls, and over 5 billion pages of mortgage-related documentation, and can evaluate loans against guidelines from more than 45 institutional investors, including Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, FHA, and major banks. Loan officer teams and banks can simply log into their ChatGPT Enterprise account, download the Tinman AI credit decision engine app, connect their guidelines, pricing, and CRM to process, underwrite, and fulfill loans nearly instantly; passing thousands of dollars in savings to consumers.

item.title: Vertafore introduces Velocity AI Platform and AI agents to power Distribution Velocity for the Insurance Industry
item.description: The post Vertafore introduces Velocity AI Platform and AI agents to power Distribution Velocity for the Insurance Industry appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . InsurTech leader embeds AI across the insurance lifecycle to help agencies, MGAs and carriers accelerate workflows, improve decisions and eliminate distribution drag Today at Accelerate 2026, Vertafore ® introduced its Velocity AI Platform and six AI agents that deliver speed and improve precision in everyday insurance workflows. “What we’re delivering with Velocity AI is a practical way to bring AI into the workflows that drive this industry forward, so our customers can move faster, make better decisions and focus on relationships and the activities that matter most.” AI agents designed for insurance workflows Built on the Velocity AI Platform , Vertafore announced its new portfolio of Velocity AI agents embedded within the solutions agencies, MGAs and carriers already trust, helping to reduce manual work, improve accuracy and move processes forward more efficiently.

item.title: PURE grows to $3B as reciprocal model delivers member returns
item.description: The structure reflects PURE’s reciprocal model, where policyholders share in favorable outcomes. PURE said it will continue investing in digital tools and AI to improve service and efficiency, while maintaining a human-led model focused on member experience. PURE Insurance reported continued growth and underwriting discipline in 2025, with membership surpassing 120,000 and total premium under management exceeding $3 billion, up more than 15% year over year.

item.title: Data centres to add US$10bn premiums in 2026 amid looming protection gap: S&P
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sompo names Daniel Lukas APAC head of personal lines
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA bans CMC's misleading adverts
item.description: Adverts which used edited, unauthorised clips of Martin Lewis to make misleading claims about average motor finance compensation and used the FCA logo without permission, have been banned by the FCA. Following FCA action, CMCs have removed or amended 899 misleading adverts since January 2024. Conclusive has since removed the banned adverts.

item.title: AI Drives Europe’s Second Straight Quarter Of Funding Gain As Deal Volume Falls Sharply
item.description: As was the case globally and in North America , the main driver was AI, which for the first time claimed more than 50% of Europe’s total funding for the quarter. Still, Europe saw more capital going into fewer companies in Q1, with deal volume plummeting 40% year over year. Funding to Europe-based AI startups increased significantly last quarter, reaching $9.2 billion, or more than half of total venture funding to the region.

item.title: Arch Capital’s Voussoir Re sidecar issues and lists 2026-3 preferred shares
item.description: We understand the preferred shares have become the preferred way for Arch Capital to enable investors to access reinsurance-linked returns through its Voussoir Re sidecar structure. The Voussoir Re sidecar vehicle became a key third-party capital structure for Arch since the structures launch in 2019 . In 2019, we reported on the first $74 million issuance of notes from Voussoir Re Ltd., which was a quota share based reinsurance sidecar style arrangement for Arch Capital.

item.title: Catastrophe bonds highlighted as a critical tool for impact-focused fixed income
item.description: However, a recent article argues that investors are overlooking significant opportunities within the space, highlighting catastrophe bonds as a fundamental pillar of impact-driven fixed income. The article comes from David Foye, a Sustainable Finance consultant at the Environmental Defense Fund, who outlined that as climate-driven disasters continue to grow more frequent and severe, a persistent protection gap continues to remain between insured and uninsured losses, which has led to catastrophe bonds being highlighted as a “critical tool” for managing climate-related risks at scale. In the article Foye, states that cat bonds deserve a “closer look”; not for being a particularly niche financial product, but as an underappreciated form of impact-oriented fixed income.

item.title: Brace promotes Wheeler and Davis as regional Heads of Underwriting for US and London
item.description: Brace Underwriting Limited, a multi-line and alternative risk specialist underwriter backed by Octave Specialty Group Inc., has promoted Richard Wheeler to Head of Underwriting for USA, and Michael Davis to Head of Brace London Underwriting. Under the new structure, Martin McBrearty remains Head of Brace UK and Europe, completing a team of three regional underwriting heads reporting to Tom Sambrook, Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO). Their appointments reflect a new management structure designed to support Brace’s expansion across its underwriting platforms.

item.title: PwC highlights growing role of asset-intensive reinsurance in Cayman market
item.description: PwC has highlighted the growing role of asset-intensive reinsurance (AIR) in shaping the Cayman Islands into a key hub for capital-efficient, asset-driven reinsurance solutions. Against this backdrop of rapid expansion, PwC suggested that AIR is set to play an increasingly pivotal role as insurers and reinsurers shift toward more capital-efficient, asset-driven models in an increasingly complex and competitive market. Supported by regulatory stability, strong capital markets, and a commitment to innovation, PwC noted that the Cayman Islands has cemented its position as a dynamic reinsurance centre.

item.title: EIOPA & ESM propose European risk-sharing mechanism to reduce nat cat protection gap
item.description: A paper by the European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority (EIOPA) and the European Stability Mechanism (ESM) proposes a European-level risk-sharing mechanism comprising a natural catastrophe insurance pool and a loan-based backstop, aimed at enhancing overall risk-bearing capacity and reducing Europe’s significant protection gap. ESM and EIOPA outlined how a European-level risk-sharing mechanism could deliver significant diversification benefits and enable more cost-effective funding, crowding in the private sector. The risk-sharing mechanism would also include a loan-based backstop for extreme tail events that exceed the pool’s capacity.

item.title: US, Iran May Resume Talks This Week Despite Port Blockade
item.description: and Iran could return to Islamabad this week to resume talks to end the war, sources told Reuters on Tuesday, after the collapse of weekend negotiations prompted Washington to impose a blockade on Iranian ports. The highest-level talks between the two adversaries since the 1979 Islamic Revolution ended in the Pakistani capital without a breakthrough at the weekend, raising doubts over the survival of a two-week ceasefire that still has a week to run. But a source involved in the talks said on Tuesday both countries could return as early as the end of this week, and that a proposal had been shared with Washington and Tehran to resend their delegations.

item.title: Tankers Pass Strait of Hormuz on First Day of US Blockade, Data Shows
item.description: Another sanctioned tanker, Rich Starry, would be the first to make it through the strait and to exit the Gulf since the blockade began, data from LSEG and Kpler showed. A third Iran-linked tanker was entering the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday on the first full day of the U.S. blockade on vessels calling at Iranian ports, shipping data showed.

item.title: Canadian Insurers Push Owners to Fortify Homes, Urge Carney to Prioritize Climate Risks
item.description: Worried about wildfires sending claims soaring this year, Canada’s property and casualty insurers are pushing owners to flood- and fire-proof homes and urging the government to take climate issues more seriously despite economic turmoil. They worry Prime Minister Mark Carney’s prioritization of energy and the economy over risks from climate change could contribute to more wildfire- and flood-related damage over time. Insurers Intact Financial, TD Insurance, Wawanesa and Definity Financial face financial pressure as claims surge and in turn push up home-insurance premiums, which rose about 6% last year in Canada.

item.title: Flock launches haulage fleet insurance with Admiral to expand connected model in UK
item.description: The post Flock launches haulage fleet insurance with Admiral to expand connected model in UK appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Flock has launched a haulage fleet insurance product in partnership with Admiral, marking its first major expansion into the UK haulage segment. The move extends Flock’s connected insurance model into one of the UK’s largest commercial vehicle markets, combining its data-driven risk approach with Admiral’s underwriting and claims capabilities.

item.title: Westfield Health launches Evolve to digitise health cash plans as revenue nears $125m
item.description: The post Westfield Health launches Evolve to digitise health cash plans as revenue nears $125m appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Westfield Health is on track to surpass £100m (approximately $125m) in annual revenue for the first time, as it launches Evolve, a fully digital platform designed to transform how intermediaries quote, sell, and manage health cash plans. Evolve brings together health cash plans and private health insurance into a single, flexible offering, allowing intermediaries to deliver both everyday healthcare support and higher-value protection through one platform.

item.title: Old Republic spins out Lodestar as standalone TPA brand to sharpen market focus
item.description: Old Republic International has launched Lodestar Claims & Risk Services as a standalone third-party administrator (TPA) brand, formally separating the business from PMA Companies where it previously operated as a division. The move gives Lodestar its own market identity for the first time in more than three decades, while it remains part of Old Republic’s broader operating structure. Positioned as a top 10 national TPA, Lodestar provides claims administration and risk services to middle-market and large employers, carriers, and distribution partners across all 50 US states.

item.title: GEICO partners with Dallas Wings
item.description: GEICO has signed a multi-year partnership with the Dallas Wings , expanding its sports marketing presence in North Texas with a focus on women’s sports, youth programs, and community initiatives. As part of the deal, GEICO will integrate into game-day experiences, digital content, and team storytelling, while also serving as presenting partner of the Dallas Wings Youth Basketball Summer Camps and supporting regional community efforts. We look forward to working with GEICO for many years to come.” – Dallas Wings CEO and Managing Partner Greg Bibb.

item.title: The Hartford and UConn launch collaboration to advance business resiliency and worker safety
item.description: The post The Hartford and UConn launch collaboration to advance business resiliency and worker safety appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Through this strategic relationship with UConn, we are combining academic research with real-world risk expertise to generate practical insights employers can use to improve worker safety, reduce business losses and maintain productivity as climate-driven exposures continue to evolve.” – The Hartford’s President A. The Hartford and The University of Connecticut announced the initial phases of a collaboration focused on research related to energy innovation, business resiliency and extreme heat.

item.title: Comparion partners with 23XI Racing and Tyler Reddick for 2026 NASCAR season
item.description: Comparion Insurance Agency a subsidiary of Liberty Mutual , has entered a sponsorship partnership with 23XI Racing and driver Tyler Reddick for the 2026 NASCAR season. 23XI Racing, co-owned by Michael Jordan and Denny Hamlin , fields multiple drivers including Reddick and Bubba Wallace . The deal positions Comparion as an official partner of Reddick, who drives the No.

item.title: OpenAI IDs Security Issue Involving Third-Party Tool, Says User Data Was Not Accessed
item.description: OpenAI said on Friday it had identified a security issue involving a third-party developer tool called Axios and is taking steps to protect the process that certifies its macOS applications are legitimate OpenAI apps. Passwords and OpenAI API keys were not affected by the third-party security issue, the company said, adding that the root cause of the security incident was a misconfiguration in the GitHub Actions workflow, which has been addressed. The ChatGPT maker said it found no evidence that its user data was accessed, that its systems or intellectual property was compromised, or that its software was altered.

item.title: Ex-First Brands Officer Says He Was Kept in the Dark About Fraud
item.description: First Brands Group’s former chief strategy officer asked a judge to dismiss a civil lawsuit that blames him in part for the auto-parts supplier’s collapse, arguing that founder Patrick James and his brother, Edward, concealed the alleged fraud that brought down the company. Michael Baker, who joined First Brands after practicing corporate finance in New York, said in an April 6 court filing that Patrick James and other insiders used his reputation and experience to build relationships with lenders. Related: Convicted Insurance Mogul Lindberg Should Pay $1.6B Restitution to Companies A spokesperson for Patrick James said in an email Thursday that former First Brands officers had a duty to raise concerns at the time and are now trying to “shift responsibility in a manner that serves their own interests.” “What has happened to the business of First Brands is a tragedy, but the narrative that the blame for this should fall on Patrick James is false,” his spokesperson said, adding that James looks forward to presenting his case in court.

item.title: US Trade Chief Says Tech Restrictions to Block Chinese Carmakers
item.description: The top US trade official said restrictions on foreign technology will likely keep Chinese carmakers out of the US for the foreseeable future, throwing cold water on the prospect of those manufacturers establishing a foothold in the domestic auto market. Rules prohibiting connected vehicle technology and software made by so-called foreign entities of concern pose a steep barrier for many Chinese companies, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told reporters Thursday. “It would probably be difficult for certain countries to establish new production here, given those sets of rules.” Related: Xiaomi Launches Safety Advisory Committee After EV Accidents in China The prospect of Chinese carmakers entering the US market has become a closely watched topic for the domestic auto industry this year, particularly after President Donald Trump indicated in January that he would be open to arrangements that employed American workers.

item.title: Data Centers Offer a Potential $10 Billion Windfall for Insurers
item.description: The widespread adoption of artificial intelligence in recent years had propelled demand for new, larger data centers. Today, the 11,000 data centers in operation globally already represent more than $2 trillion of insurable value. For instance, business interruption is more complex for data centers, in part because of their interconnectedness with other campuses.
